name: Cheese Enchiladas with Green Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 can diced green chilies (4 ounces), drained
recipeInstructions:
1. For the sauce: Cook spinach according to package instructions. Drain well and set aside.
Melt butter in a heavy medium skillet over medium heat. Add flour and stir mixture for 2 minutes; do not brown.
Gradually whisk in whipping cream and milk. Simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in spinach, cilantro, green onion, chilies, cumin, coriander, and red pepper. Puree in batches in a processor until almost smooth.
Season with salt and pepper. (The sauce can be prepared up to one day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Bring to room temperature before using.)
2. For the enchiladas: Heat oil in a heavy small skillet over medium-high heat. Briefly dip each tortilla in oil to soften, about 15 seconds per side.
Transfer to paper towels and drain. Combine cheeses in a large bowl; set aside 1 1/2 cups for topping.
Combine onion and cilantro in a small bowl. Place 1/4 cup cheese mixture in the center of one tortilla.
Spoon 2 teaspoons onion mixture over it. Roll up the tortilla. Place seam side down in a large glass baking dish.
Repeat with remaining tortillas, cheese, and onion, using 1/4 cup cheese for each. (The enchiladas can be made up to one day ahead. Cover and chill.)
3. Preheat oven to 375°F. Stir sour cream into sauce; pour over enchiladas. Sprinkle with the reserved 1 1/2 cups cheese.
Bake until cheese melts and enchiladas are heated through, about 25 minutes.
